notes_from_pm: formerly XII
doorways in outer part usurped from Ramesses V.
Inaccessible after Corridor B.
Temp. Merneptaḥ-Siptaḥ.
wife of Sethos II. Usurped by Setnakht.
formerly XI
son of Ramesses IX.
Excavated by Davis, and by Carnarvon and Carter.
Excavated by Belzoni.
1st ed. 24
Standard-bearer, Child of the nursery. Temp. Ḥatshepsut. Excavated by Loret.
Excavated by Loret.

Uninscribed tomb, attributed to Amenophis I by Weigall in Ann. Serv. xi (1911), pp. 174-5 [12], and id. A Guide to the Antiquities of Upper Egypt, pp. 163-4, but this is not supported by any inscriptional evidence, and does not correspond with the position given in the Abbott Papyrus (cf. Peet, The Great Tomb-Robberies of the Twentieth Egyptian Dynasty, pp. 37-8). See also the tomb of Queen ʿAhmosi Nefertere, infra, p. 599.
(?). Excavated by Loret
Excavated by Davis.
Overseer of the Fields of Amūn, Dyn. XVIII, re-used by Merenkhons, Doorkeeper of the House of Amūn, Dyn. XXII (name from scarab). Excavated by Davis and Carter.
Divine father, and Chief of the harîm of Amūn, parents of Queen Teye.
Governor of the town, Vizier. Temp. Amenophis II. (Also owner of Theb. tb. 29.)
Probably Amenophis IV, formerly attributed to Queen Teye or to Smenkhkarēʿ.
'Gold Tomb', uninscribed.
1st ed. 58. Excavated by Carnarvon and Carter.
Dyn. XX(?). Buried. (L. D. Text, No. 14, Bibl. i, 1st ed. No. 37.)
A PRINCESS, no name.
wife of Ramesses I. Unfinished. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 7, L. D. Text, No. 13, WILKINSON, No. 19.)
A QUEEN, cartouche blank. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 6, L. D. Text, No. 12, WILKINSON, No. 18.)
Charioteer of the stable of the Great House, son of Ramesses III. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 8, L. D. Text, No. 11, WILKINSON, No. 16, HAY, No. 1.)
King's son, Hereditary prince of the royal children of his Majesty, Charioteer of the Great Stable, &c., son of Ramesses III.
Sem-priest of Ptaḥ, son of Ramesses III.
(probably). Vizier. Temp. Tuthmosis I. (Bibl. i, 1st ed. p. 49.)
daughter of Seḳenenreʿ-Taʿa and Sit-ḏḥout. Dyn. XVII. (Bibl. i, 1st ed. p. 49.)
mother of Ramesses VI, daughter of Ḥubalzanet (i.e. Ḥemzert). (CHAMPOLLION, No. 4, L. D. Text, No. 10, WILKINSON, No. 14.)
Ramesside. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 3, L. D. Text, No. 9, WILKINSON, No. 12, HAY, No. 2.)
son of Ramesses III. (L. D. Text, No. 8, WILKINSON, No. 11, HAY, No. 3.)
Royal scribe, Overseer of horses, son of Ramesses III.
(?). daughter of Ramesses II. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 11, L. D. Text, No. 6, HAY, 2nd tomb east.)
wife of Ramesses II.
daughter of Ramesses II. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 12, L. D. Text, No. 5, HAY, No. 4.)
daughter of Ramesses II. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 13, L. D. Text, No. 4, HAY, No. 5.)
A PRINCESS, no name. Dyn. XX. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 14, L. D. Text, No. 3, HAY, No. 6.)
Great King's mother and King's wife. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 15, L. D. Text, No. 2, HAY, No. 7.)
A QUEEN, no name. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 16, L. D. Text, No. 1, HAY, No. 8.)
Servant in the Place of Truth. Dyn. XIX. Father, Khaʿbekhnet (name on fragment, BRUYÈRE, Rapport (1927), fig. 34 [4]). Wife, Iyneferti.
Servant in the Place of Truth. Temp. Ramesses II. (L. D. Text, No. 107.) Parents, Sennezem (tomb 1) and Iyneferti. Wives, Saḥte and (probably) Esi.
Servant in the Place of Truth on the west of Thebes. Ramesside. Parents, Menna and Huy. Wife, Nezemtbehdet.
Chiseller of Amūn in the Place of Truth. Temp. Ramesses II. (L. D. Text, No. 106.) Parents, Thonūfer, Chiseller of Amūn in the Khenu, and Maʿetnefert. Wives, Nefertere and Ḥenutmehyt.
Servant in the Place of Truth on the west of Thebes. Ramesside. Parents, Neferronpet and Mahi (name on stela in Brit. Mus. 150, see infra, p. 14). Wife, Taēsi.
Foremen in the Place of Truth. Temp. Ḥaremḥab to Ramesses II. (L. D. Text, No. 101.) Wife (of Neferḥōtep), Iymau; (of Nebnūfer), Iy.
Scribe in the Place of Truth. Temp. Ramesses II. (L. D. Text, No. 99.) Parents, Amenemḥab and Kakaia. Wife, Mutemwia.
Chief in the Great Place. Temp. Amenophis II, Tuthmosis IV, and Amenophis III. (L. D. Text, No. 96.) Wife, Meryt.
Servant in the Place of Truth, Charmer of scorpions. Ramesside. Wife, Tent-hōm.

Servants in the Place of Truth. Temp. Ramesses II. (L. D. Text, No. 97.) Father (of Penbuy), Iri (name from offering-table of Penbuy, in Turin Mus. 1559). Wives (of Penbuy), Amentetusert and Irnūfer; (of Kasa), Bukhaʿnef.
Overseer of the treasury, Overseer of works. Temp. Ḥatshepsut to Tuthmosis III. (L. D. Text, No. 1.) Mother, Dediu.
Overseer of the granary of the King's wife and King's mother ʿAḥḥotp. Temp. Amosis to Amenophis I (?). (CHAMPOLLION, No. 51, L. D. Text, No. 2.) Mother, ʿAḥmosi.
Head of brazier-bearers of Amūn. Ramesside. Wife, Wernūfer.
wʿab-priest of 'Amenophis, the favourite of Amūn'. Ramesside.
King's son, Mayor in the Southern City. Early Dyn. XVIII. Parents, Raḥotp, Overseer of the harim of the Lake (i.e. Fayûm), and Sensonb. Wife, Senbi.
Prophet of 'Amenophis of the Forecourt'. Temp. Ramesses II. Wife, Ternūte.
Scribe and physician of the King. Temp. Amenophis II (?). Parents, Nebseny, Judge, and Amenḥotp (?). Wife, Ta...nūfer.
Chief servant who weighs the silver and gold of the estate of Amūn. Temp. Tuthmosis III or earlier. Father, a Scribe of counting of cattle of Queen ʿAḥmosi Nefertere. Wife, Mosi.
First prophet of 'Amenophis of the Forecourt'. Temp. Ramesses I to Sethos I (?). Wife, Iuy, Chief of the harim of Amenophis.
Fan-bearer, Mayor of Aphroditopolis. Temp. Tuthmosis III (?). Mother, Taysent.
Scribe, Steward of Tuthmosis I. Temp. Tuthmosis I. Wife, Bakt.
Royal butler. Partly usurped by Mery[amūn], Eldest son of the King. Temp. Tuthmosis III(?). Wife (of Mery[amūn]), Ḥatshepsut.

Royal scribe of the dispatches of the Lord of the Two Lands. Temp. Merneptaḥ. (L. D. Text, No. 38.) Parents, Khaʿemteri, Scribe of soldiers, and Tamy. Wives, Raʿya, Chief of the harim of Sobk, and Nebttaui.
Steward of the royal wife Nebtu. Temp. Tuthmosis III. Parents, Tetires and Ipu. Wife, Resti.
First prophet of Khons. Ramesside. Wife, Tausert, Chief of the harim of Khons.
Overseer of the treasury in the Ramesseum in the estate of Amūn. Temp. Ramesses II. (L. D. Text, No. 29.) Wife, Meryēsi.
Chief steward of the divine adoratress ʿAnkhnesneferebreʿ. Temp. Apries and Amasis. (Inaccessible.) Parents, Ḥarsiēsi, Chamberlain of the divine adoratress, and Tahibet (from cone).
Officer of the estate of Amūn. Ramesside.
Governor of the town, Vizier. Temp. Amenophis II. (HAY, No. 15.) Parents, [ʿAḥmosi] Ḥumay (tomb 224) and Nub. Wife, Wertmaʿetef.
Scribe of the treasury of the estate of Amūn. Ramesside. Wife, Ḥenutenkhunet.

First prophet of Menkheperreʿ (Tuthmosis III). Temp. Ramesses II. (L. D. Text, No. 51.) Parents, Neferḥotep, First prophet of Amenophis II, and Tausert, Songstress of Monthu. Wives, Ruia and Mutia or May.
Chief steward of Amūn, Overseer of the granaries of Upper and Lower Egypt. Temp. Ramesses II. Wife, Esi.
Prophet, Chief lector. Saite. (L. D. Text, No. 20.) Mother, Namenkhesi, Sistrum-player of Amūn. Wife, Tedi.

Fourth prophet of Amūn in Thebes. Temp. Taharqa and Psammetikhos I. Parents, Espṭaḥ, Prophet of Amūn, Mayor of the City, and Esenkhebi. Wives, Uzarenes, Sistrum-player of Amen-Reʿ (name in tomb), Eskhons and Shepetenmut (names from cones).

First prophet of Amūn. Temp. Ramesses II. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 45, L. D. Text, Nos. 10, 11.) Parents, Roma, First and second prophet of Amūn, and Roma, Singer of Amūn. Wife, Mertesger, Chief of the harim of Amūn.

Chief steward of the divine adoratress. Temp. Psammetikhos I. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 56, L. D. Text, No. 25.) Parents, ʿAnkh-ḥor, Divine father, and De-ubasteiri, variant Teiri. Wife, Shepenernute (name in tomb 196).

Chief steward of the god's wife Amenardais I. Saite. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 54, L. D. Text, No. 23.) Parents, Pedemut, Scribe, and Estawert (names from statues, in Berlin Mus. 8163, see infra, p. 69, and Cairo Mus. Ent. 36711).
Scribe, Counter of the grain in the granary of divine offerings of Amūn. Temp. Tuthmosis IV. Wife, Wazronpet.
Second prophet of Amūn. Temp. Tuthmosis III. (L. D. Text, No. 18.) Parents, Puia and Neferi. Wives, Tanefert and Sensonb.
Viceroy of Kush, Governor of the South Lands. Temp. Amenophis IV to Tutʿankhamūn. (CHAMPOLLION, A, L. D. Text, No. 110.) Mother, Wenḥo.
Chief steward of Amūn in the Southern City. Temp. Ramesses I to Sethos I(?). (CHAMPOLLION, No. 35.) Parents, Nefertiu, Judge, and Iny, Songstress of the Theban Triad. Wife, Nezem(t).
Captain of troops, Eyes of the King in the Two Lands of the Retenu. Temp. Tuthmosis III to Amenophis II. Wife, Ḥenuttaui.
Overseer of the kitchen of the Lord of the Two Lands. Temp. Amenophis II(?).
wab-priest-in-front of Amūn. Ramesside. Father, Ḥori, wab-priest of Amūn. Wife, Isumut.

Steward of the First prophet of Amūn, Mery (tomb 95), temp. Amenophis II. Usurped by Ḏḥutemḥab, Head of the makers of fine linen(?) of the estate of Amūn, temp. Ramesses II (?). Mother (of Ḏḥout), Ḏḥout. Parents (of Ḏḥutemḥab), Unnūfer, Head of weavers of the Temple of Amūn, and Ḥsi. Wife (of Ḏḥutemḥab), Bek-khons, Songstress of the Theban Triad.
Steward, Overseer of the granaries of Upper and Lower Egypt. Temp. Amenophis III (?). Wife, Nefertkhaʿ.
Overseer of the royal harim. Temp. Amenophis III. (Inaccessible.) Parents, Neḥ, Judge, and Senenu. Wife, Maiay.
Chief steward, At the head of the King, Overseer of the cattle of Amūn. Temp. Amenophis III. Parents, Ith-taui, Overseer of the cattle of Amūn, and Mut-tuy, Royal concubine.
Chief scribe of Amūn. Probably temp. Ay. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 53, HAY, No. 11.) Parents, Neby, Servant of Amūn, and Iuy. Wife, Merytreʿ.
Divine father of Amen-Reʿ. Temp. Ḥaremḥab. Parents, Amenemonet, Divine father of Amūn, and Takhaʿt, Chief of the harim of Amūn. Wife, Rennutet.
First prophet of the royal ka of Tuthmosis I. Temp. Sethos I. Parents, Khensemḥab and Tausert, Songstress of Monthu. Wife, Ḥatshepsut called Shepset.
Scribe, Astronomer of Amūn. Temp. Tuthmosis IV(?). Wife, Tawi.
Agent of Amūn. Temp. Tuthmosis III. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 16 bis, L. D. Text, No. 78.) Parents, Yotefnūfer, Agent of Amūn, and Tetiemnūter. Wife, Sebknakht.

Sculptor of Amūn, temp. Tuthmosis IV to Amenophis III(?). Usurped by Kenro, wab-priest, Head of the magazine of Khons, early Dyn. XIX. Wife (of Ḥuy), Taenheruensi, (of Kenro), Tarenenu, Chief of the harim of Amūn.
Governor of the town and Vizier. Temp. Amenophis IV. Parents, Neby, Overseer of the cattle of Amūn in the Northern District, and Ipuia. Wife, Mery(t)ptaḥ.
Royal scribe, Child of the nursery. Temp. Amenophis II. (L. D. Text, No. 81.) Wife, Mutnefert, Royal concubine.
Royal scribe, Overseer of the granaries of Upper and Lower Egypt. Temp. Amenophis III. (L. D. Text, No. 80.) Wife, Tiyi.

Name unknown, temp. Amenophis III. Usurped by Amenḥotp, Overseer of the prophets of Amūn, and his son Amenemōnet, Temple-scribe of the Temple of Ramesses 'Beloved like Amūn', Dyn. XX. (L. D. Text, No. 43.) Wife (of Amenemōnet), Ḥenutʿanensu.
First prophet of Mut, Mistress of Asher. Early Dyn. XVIII. Parents, ...n, Overseer of the granary, and Tuiu, Royal concubine. Wife, Meryt.
Governor of the town and Vizier, and mother, Sent, Prophetess of Ḥatḥor. Temp. Sesostris I. (L. D. Text, No. 42.) Wife (of Antefoḳer), Sitsisobk. Mother (of Sent), Dui.
Governor of the town and Vizier. Temp. Tuthmosis III. Parents, ʿAmethu (tomb 83) and Taʿamethu. Wife, Thuiu. (L. D. Text, No. 44.)
Overseer of the Cabinet. Temp. Tuthmosis III (?).

Mayor of the Southern Lake and the Lake of Sobk. Temp. Tuthmosis IV. Father, Min, Overseer of the seal. Wife, Meryt, Nurse of the King's daughter Tiʿa, Chief of the harim of Sobk of Shedty. (L. D. Text, No. 48.)
Nurse of the King's son Amenḥotp. Temp. Tuthmosis IV. Father, Ḥeḳreshu, Tutor of the King's son. (L. D. Text, No. 47.)

Scribe of the royal accounts (?) in the Presence, Overseer of the granary, temp. Ḥatshepsut (?). Usurped by Imiseba, Head of the altar, Head of the temple-scribes of the estate of Amūn, temp. Ramesses IX. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 60, L. D. Text, No. 40, WILKINSON, No. 1, 'Aichesi' of Prisse.) Parents, Amenḥotp, Head of scribes of the Temple of Amen-reʿ in Karnak, and Mutemmeres. Wife, Te(n)tpapersetha.
Vizier. Temp. Tuthmosis IV. Wife, Rennai.
First prophet of Amūn. Temp. Ḥatshepsut. Parents, Ḥepu, Third lector of Amūn, and ʿAḥḥotp, Royal concubine. Wife, Amenḥotp (name from Silsila, L. D. iii. 28 [4 b]). (L. D. Text, No. 50.)

wʿab-priest of Amūn of Karnak, and of Mut of Asher, Dyn. XX. Usurped by Espaneferḥor, Head of the temple-scribes of the estate of Amūn, temp. Siamūn. Father (of Espaneferḥor), Iufenamūn. Wife (of Espaneferḥor), Tabekenmut, Singer of the ... of Mut.
Scribe of the fields of the Lord of the Two Lands of Upper and Lower Egypt. Temp. Tuthmosis IV (?). Wife, Ḥenuttaui.
Usurped by Amenmosi, Overseer of sandal-makers (?) of the estate of Amūn. Dyn. XXI. Father, Suʿawiamūn, same title. Wife, Kefenu.
Chief steward, Steward of Amūn. Temp. Ḥatshepsut. (L. D. Text, No. 46.) Parents, Raʿmosi and Ḥatnefer(t).
First prophet of Amūn in the Mortuary Temple of Tuthmosis III. Temp. Amenophis II. (L. D. Text, No. 45.) Parents, ʿAḥmosi, [First] prophet of Amūn, and Reʿy, Royal concubine.
Amenḥotp (?), Overseer of works on the two great obelisks in the Temple of Amūn, Chief steward, Veteran of the King. Temp. Ḥatshepsut.
Royal scribe, Commander of soldiers. Temp. Tuthmosis IV. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 3, L. D. Text, No. 53.) Wife, Mutiriy, Songstress of Thoth and Neḥemʿawat.
Second prophet of Amūn. Temp. Tuthmosis IV. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 2, L. D. Text, No. 54, HAY, No. 4.) Mother, Paʿa. Wife, Roy.
Fan-bearer on the right of the King. Temp. Tuthmosis IV. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 1, WILKINSON, No. 17, L. D. Text, No. 55.) Wife, Nebttaui.

Child of the nursery, Overseer of works in the Temple of Amūn, Standard-bearer of the Lord of the Two Lands. Usurped by Roy, Overseer of sculptors of the Lord of the Two Lands. Temp. Tuthmosis IV. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 8 bis, L. D. Text, No. 62.) Wife (of Ptaḥemḥet), Meryt. Wife (of Roy), Raʿḥuy.
Royal scribe, Scribe of recruits. Temp. Tuthmosis III to Amenophis III. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 4, L. D. Text, No. 57, WILKINSON, No. 16, HAY, No. 23.) Mother, Ēsi. Wife, Ithuy.

Overseer of the granary of the Lord of the Two Lands, wʿab-priest in the Mortuary Temple of Tuthmosis III. Temp. Tuthmosis III to Amenophis II (?). (CHAMPOLLION, No. 7, L. D. Text, No. 60.) Father, Minnakht (tomb 87).
Overseer of the treasury, Royal scribe. Temp. Amenophis II. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 6, L. D. Text, No. 59, HAY, No. 21.) Wife, Takhaʿt.

[1st ed. Anena], Overseer of the granary of Amūn. Temp. Amenophis I to Tuthmosis III. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 5, WILKINSON, No. 14, HAY, No. 8.) Parents, Ineni, Judge, and Sit-ḏhout. Wife, ʿAḥḥotp, called Thuiu.
Scribe, Counter of the grain of Amūn, Steward of the Vizier. Temp. Tuthmosis III. (L. D. Text, No. 56, HAY, No. 16.) Parents, Ḏhutmosi, Overseer of lands, and Antef. Wife, Beketamūn.
Governor of the town and Vizier. Early temp. Tuthmosis III. (L. D. Text, No. 41, WILKINSON, No. 64.) Wife, Taʿamethu (name in tomb 131).

First royal herald, Overseer of the gate, temp. Tuthmosis III. Partly usurped by Mery (tomb 95), temp. Amenophis II. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 11, L. D. Text, No. 71, WILKINSON, No. 31, HAY, No. 19.) Parents (of Amunezeḥ), Siḏhout, Judge, and Resi. Wife (of Amunezeḥ), Ḥenutnefert.
Lieutenant-commander of soldiers. Temp. Tuthmosis III to Amenophis II. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 12, HAY, No. 20.) Mother, Tetires. Wife, Baki, Chief royal nurse.
First prophet of Amūn. Temp. Tuthmosis III. Parents, Amenemḥēt and Taōnet, King's nurse.
Overseer of the granaries of Upper and Lower Egypt, Overseer of horses of the Lord of the Two Lands, Royal scribe. Temp. Tuthmosis III. (HAY, No. 17.) Father, Sen-ḏhout.

Lieutenant of the King, Standard-bearer of the Lord of the Two Lands. Temp. Tuthmosis III to Amenophis II. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 8, L. D. Text, No. 61.) Wife, Neit, Chief royal nurse, Governess of the god.
Steward in the Southern City. Temp. Amenophis III.

Standard-bearer of (the sacred bark called) 'Beloved-of-Amūn', Captain of troops of the police on the west of Thebes. Temp. Tuthmosis IV to Amenophis III. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 9 bis, L. D. Text, No. 63, HAY, No. 22.) Wives, Sensenbut and Tiy.
Captain of the troops ... , Overseer of horses. Temp. Tuthmosis IV to Amenophis III. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 8 ter, L. D. Text, No. 65, HAY, No. 13.)
Royal butler clean of hands. Temp. Amenophis II. (L. D. Text, No. 67.) Parents, Iamnūfer, Mayor of Neferusi, and Meryt, Songstress of Thoth. Wife, Ḥat.
Chief steward of the King. Temp. Amenophis II. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 8 quater, L. D. Text, No. 68, WILKINSON, No. 33, HAY, No. 18.) Mother, Amenemōpet, Royal nurse. Wife, Tadedetes.
called ʿAmy, First royal herald, Fan-bearer on the right of the King. Temp. Amenophis II (?). Mother, Sent.

First prophet of Amūn. (See also usurpation in tomb 84.) Temp. Amenophis II. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 14, L. D. Text, No. 70, HAY, No. 14.) Parents, Nebpeḥtireʿ, First prophet of Min of Koptos, and Ḥunay(t), Chief nurse of the Lord of the Two Lands (name from tomb 84). Wife, Dey.

Mayor of the Southern City. Temp. Amenophis II. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 13, WILKINSON, No. 34, HAY, No. 12.) Parents, [ʿAḥmosi] Ḥumay (tomb 224) and Nub. Wives, Sentnay and Sentnefert, Royal nurses, and Meryt.
First prophet of Amūn. Temp. Amenophis II (?). Father, Ḏḥutiḥotp, wʿab-priest, Overseer of sandal-makers of the Temple of Amūn.
Third prophet of Amūn. Temp. Tuthmosis III to Amenophis II (?). (CHAMPOLLION, No. 9, L. D. Text, No. 69.) (Inaccessible.) Mother, a Chief nurse of the Lord of the Two Lands. Wife, Ḥenuttaui.
Overseer of the seal, Overseer of the gold-land of Amūn. Temp. Tuthmosis III. Mother, Sit-ḏḥout. Wife, Taimau.

Governor of the town and Vizier. Temp. Tuthmosis III to Amenophis II. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 15, L. D. Text, No. 58, WILKINSON, No. 35.) Parents, Neferweben, [Vizier], wʿab-priest of Amūn, and Bet. Wife, Meryt.
[Royal butler] clean of hands. Temp. Amenophis II.
Royal scribe, Child of the nursery. Temp. Amenophis III. (Inaccessible.)
Governor of the town and Vizier. End of Dyn. XI. (L. D. Text, No. 36.) Wife (or mother), Maʿetnemti.
(See tomb 80.) Temp. Amenophis II.
Prophet of the noble ram-sceptre of Amūn. Dyn. XIX. Wife, Amenemopet.

Governor of the town and Vizier. Temp. Sethos I to Ramesses II. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 32, L. D. Text, No. 39, HAY, No. 7.) Parents, Nebneteru called Theri, Chief prophet of Amūn, and Merytreʿ, Chief of the harim of Amūn. Wife, Tiy, Chief of the harim of Amūn.
Royal scribe, Steward of the estate of Amenophis III 'Reʿ is brilliant'. Temp. Amenophis III. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 33, L. D. Text, No. 37.) Parents, Neby, Judge, and Ḥepu.
First prophet of Onuris. Temp. Tuthmosis IV(?). Wife, Sensonb.
Mayor of Thinis, Overseer of prophets of Onuris. Temp. Tuthmosis III. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 34.) Mother, Say.
Royal butler, Royal herald. Temp. Ḥatshepsut to Tuthmosis III. Parents, Pesediri (?) and Keku. Wife, Baktḥ.
Scribe of divine writings in the estate of Amūn. Temp. Ramesses II. Parents, Simut, Head of outline-draughtsmen, and Wiay. Wife, Iuy, Songstress of Bubastis.
Temp. Tuthmosis III. Usurped by ʿAshefytemweset, Prophet of Amūn 'Great of Majesty', Ramesside. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 59.) Father (of ʿAshefytemweset), Pentawer. Wife of (ʿAshefytemweset), Mutemwia.
wʿab-priest over-the-secrets of the estate of Amūn, Prophet in the Temple of Tuthmosis IV. Temp. Ramesses VIII. (WILKINSON, No. 2, HAY, No. 10.) Father, Bekenamtūn, wʿab-priest of Amūn. Wife, Esi.
Head of goldworkers of the estate of Amūn. Dyn. XX. Father, a wʿab-priest of Anubis.
No texts. Dyn. XIX.
Hereditary prince. Temp. Tuthmosis IV to Amenophis III (?). (CHAMPOLLION, No. 10, L. D. Text, No. 64.)
Tomb, Dyn. XI, used by Zemutefʿankh, Outline-draughtsman of the House of Gold, fashioning the gods of the estate of Amūn. Dyn. XXI-XXII.
Fan-bearer on the right of the King. Temp. Amenophis III (?).
Name lost. Temp. Ḥatshepsut to Tuthmosis III.
Second prophet of Amūn. Temp. Amenophis III. Parents, Yuia and Thuiu (tomb 46 in the Valley of the Kings). Called Maḥu in GARDINER and WEIGALL, Cat.
First lector of Amūn. Temp. Tuthmosis III (?).

with Chapels of Amenemḥet, both Overseers of the magazine of Amūn. Temp. Tuthmosis III. Parents (of [Amen]ḥotp), ʿAmethu (tomb 83) and Taʿamethu. Father (of Amenemḥet), Neferḥotep, Prophet. Wife (of Amenemḥet), Esnub.
Scribe, Overseer of the granary, Counter of bread. Temp. Tuthmosis III. (L. D. Text, No. 84.) Wife, Ḥenu(t)iri.
Overseer of the magazine of the Lord of the Two Lands, Steward of the good god Tuthmosis I. Temp. Tuthmosis I. Wife, Taerdais.
First herald, Overseer of the estate of Amūn. Temp. Ḥatshepsut. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 22, L. D. Text, No. 77.) Mother, Tarunet.
Great commander of soldiers of the estate of Amūn. Saite (?). (CHAMPOLLION, No. 23.)
Royal scribe, Overseer of all that grows. Temp. Tuthmosis III (?), usurped in Ramesside times. (L. D. Text, No. 76.) Parents, Wazmosi and ʿAḥmosi. Wives, Sensonb and Tetisonb.
Mayor of Edfu, Mayor of the City. Saite. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 24.) Father, Pedeamun.
Name lost. Temp. Tuthmosis III or IV.
Harbour-master in the Southern City. Temp. Tuthmosis III (?). Wife, Tuy.
See also Tomb 20, supra, p. 546. Sarcophagus as Queen-Consort, quartzite, in Cairo Mus. Ent. 47032.
Probably Princess Neferureʿ, daughter of Ḥatshepsut.
Temp. Tuthmosis III.
Wife of King Seḳenenreʿ-Taʿa. Found by Mariette in 1859.
Daughter of ʿAḥḥotp (wife of King Seḳenenreʿ-Taʿa).
Tomb of Queen ʿAḥmosi Nefertere (probably). Attributed to Amenophis I by Carter, later equated by Černý with 'House of Amenophis of the Garden'.
Eldest son of King Seḳenenreʿ-Taʿa and ʿAḥḥotp.
Found by Mariette in 1860. Pyramid, see Hay MSS. 29816.
Probably younger brother and successor of Antef (Sekhemreʿ-Wepmaʿet).
Royal acquaintance. Dyn. XVII. Objects found by Vassalli in 1863.
Found by Mariette in 1857. For pyramid, possibly of Kamosi, see infra, p. 620.
Wife of King Ḏḥuti. Found in tomb by Passalacqua.
Unfinished.
Scribe of the Great Harîm, probably temp. Antef (Nubkheperrēʿ). Rock-tomb, uninscribed. Found by Mariette in 1860, probably near Theb. tb. 13.
Pyramid behind Theb. tb. 24, perhaps belonging to this tomb.
